post.summary: Verify DNS for auto TLS domain

put.description:
The update_type field controls which fields are processed. Fields sent with the wrong update_type are silently ignored.

- main (default): name, description
- state: state (start/stop/archive/unarchive/schedule), backfill, scheduled_start, scheduled_stop
- recipients: type (behavioral/seg_attr/transactional/date/webhook/relationship/object), event, event_type, anchors, filters, attribute_filters, recipients_filter, date_triggered_* fields
- add_actions: actions[] (array of new actions with type), edges[]
- save_action: action{} (single action with id), edges[]
- delete_actions: action_ids[], exit_early, edges[]
- edges: edges[] (rewire connections only)
- conversion: conversion, conversion_type, conversion_segment_id, conversion_event_name, conversion_window, conversion_action
- exit_and_conversion: all conversion fields + exit_on_trigger_or_filter_not_matched, global_exit_conditions
- exit_conditions: exit_on_trigger_or_filter_not_matched, global_exit_conditions
- global_settings: send_to_unsubscribed, use_message_limits, subscription_topic_id

get.summary: Get all action metrics for campaign

get.description: Returns paginated customers filtered by email search or condition filters (mutually exclusive). Supports sorting by any attribute. Sensitive attribute filters are concealed for users without sensitive-data permission.

post.description: Queues customer creation via the ingress pipeline. Required identifiers depend on workspace identifier configuration. Identifiers are validated for format, uniqueness, and suppression status. Returns 202; the customer may not be immediately queryable.

Queues a background backfill to delete customers matching the given criteria. The type field selects the matching strategy: email, filters, customer_ids, or internal_ids. The suppress field is required and controls whether deleted customers are added to the suppression list.
Queues a customer data export. If export_relationships is true, a second relationship export is also queued. Users without sensitive-data permission receive redacted exports.
Merges the secondary customer into the primary. Exactly two customer_ids are required and primary_customer_id must be one of them. The operation is asynchronous and irreversible.
Returns the projected merged attribute set without performing the merge. Primary customer attributes take precedence; secondary attributes are added only when the primary has no value for that key.

Sets unsubscribed=false on the specified customers via the ingress pipeline. Accepts customer_ids (external) or internal_ids. In email-identified workspaces, customer_ids are interpreted as internal IDs.

Sets unsubscribed=true on the specified customers via the ingress pipeline. Accepts customer_ids (external) or internal_ids. In email-identified workspaces, customer_ids are interpreted as internal IDs.
Removes customers from the suppression list. Returns a classification of each identifier as matched (unsuppressed), not_suppressed (exists but was not suppressed), or not_found.

delete.description: Hard-deletes a single customer. Returns 204 even if the customer was already deleted (idempotent). Agent service accounts are forbidden.

get.description: Accepts external ID or internal (cio_id) format. Attributes may be redacted based on the caller's sensitive-data permission.

put.description: Queues attribute updates via the ingress pipeline. Only provided attributes are modified (merge, not replace). An empty string value deletes the attribute. Private attributes (prefixed with _) are silently stripped. Returns 202; updates may not be immediately visible.

delete.description: Removes specified devices from a customer. If no devices are specified in the body, the request is a no-op.

put.description: Queues a transactional double-opt-in email to the customer's email address. The email contains a signed link; when the recipient clicks it, edge sets cio_double_opt_in=true on the profile. Returns 202 on acceptance.
Adds or removes SMS opt-outs for a customer. Each entry requires a non-empty from field (phone number) and an optout boolean.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: External customer ID (provide this or internal_id)

parameters.name: internal_id
parameters.description: Internal customer ID (provide this or customer_id)

parameters.name: archived
parameters.description: Set to 'true' to include archived layouts

put.description:
The update_type field controls which fields are processed. Fields sent with the wrong update_type are silently ignored.

- main (default): name
- channel: channel (email/twilio/push/in_app/slack/whatsapp/line)
- recipients: segment_ids, send_percentage, send_to_unsubscribed, use_message_limits, object_type_ids, deduped, subscription_topic_id
- tracking: conversion, conversion_type, conversion_segment_id, conversion_event_name, conversion_window, conversion_action
- rate_limits: rate_limit_time_period, rate_limit_email_rate, rate_limit_spread, daily_rate_limits
- schedule: scheduled_at, timezone, tz_match_enabled
- send: (no additional fields — triggers immediate send)
- test: test_duration, test_metric
- end_test: winner_template_id, scheduled_at (optional — to schedule send to remaining)
- pause: (no additional fields)
- resume: (no additional fields)
- cancel: (no additional fields)

post.description: Returns the number of objects that have each attribute set, for all attributes of the given object type.
Returns the number of segments, campaigns, and newsletters that reference each of the specified object attributes for a given object type.

get.description: Returns detailed information about an object attribute for a given object type, including metadata, segments, campaigns, newsletters, templates, snippets, and layouts that reference it.

parameters.name: relationship
parameters.description: Whether this is a relationship attribute

post.description: Returns the number of segments, campaigns, and newsletters that reference each of the specified relationship attributes for a given object type.

get.description: Returns detailed information about a relationship attribute for a given object type, including metadata, segments, campaigns, newsletters, templates, snippets, and layouts that reference it.

parameters.name: objectTypeIds[]
parameters.description: Object type IDs to filter by

get.description: Returns all active (non-deleted) object types with their attributes, relationship attributes, object counts, and usage info.

post.description:
Creates a new object type. Required fields: name, slug, singular_name, singular_slug.
Slugs must match `^[a-zA-Z][a-zA-Z0-9_]*$`.
When the account's object type limit is reached, the type is still created but with `enabled=false` — callers must check the response to detect this.

delete.description:
Deletes an object type. Blocked if the type is in use.
Associated objects are cleaned up asynchronously via a background backfill after the type is deleted.

put.description:
Updates an object type. All fields are optional; only fields that differ from the current value are applied.
Empty strings and whitespace-only values are treated as 'not provided' and preserve the existing value.
Slug renames are blocked if the type has liquid usage. Disabling is blocked if the type is in use.
Re-enabling is blocked if the account's object type limit is reached.

get.summary: List objects by type (with filtering and pagination)
get.description:
Supports two pagination modes: continuation-based (default, descending) and page-based (activated by sending `page`).
The `name` param triggers a name search (lowercased, supports wildcards) capped at 1000 results; when this cap is hit, `total` is set to -1.
When both `name` and `filters` are provided, `name` takes priority and `filters` is ignored.
The `filters` param must be base64-encoded, URL-encoded JSON.
If the user's permissions require attribute redaction and `filters` is non-empty, the response is emptied silently.

post.description:
Queues object creation asynchronously via ingress. Returns 202 before the object is actually created.
Rejects duplicate `object_id` values synchronously. The attribute name `relationship` is reserved and cannot be used.
When `attributes` is null, the `object_id` validation is skipped and a no-attributes event is queued.
Object ID and attribute names/values are validated against environment-specific character limits.

Queues bulk object deletion via a background backfill. Three modes (by priority):
1. By filter — when `filters` or `without_relationships` is set. If both `filters` and `cio_object_ids` are provided, the IDs are ignored.
2. By internal IDs — when `cio_object_ids` is set.
3. No-op — when the body is empty, returns 204 with no side effects.
Setting only `without_relationships=true` without filters will delete all objects of this type that lack relationships.

Queues an object export for background processing. When `export_relationships` is true, a second independent export is queued.
The two exports run in separate transactions — if the relationship export fails, the object export still proceeds.

delete.description:
Deletes a single object by its CIO internal ID. Unlike get/upsert, external object IDs are not accepted.
A blank internal ID silently returns 204 with no side effects.
Creates an audit record before queuing the deletion via ingress.

get.description:
The `identifier` is first parsed as a CIO internal object ID; if that fails, it is treated as an external object ID.
An external ID that happens to match internal ID format will be resolved as an internal ID.

put.description:
Creates or updates an object asynchronously via ingress. Returns 202 before changes are applied.
Unlike the create endpoint, this does not check for duplicates — it always queues an identify event.
The `identifier` is resolved the same way as GET: internal ID first, then external ID.
The attribute name `relationship` is reserved. Object ID and attribute names/values are validated against environment-specific character limits.

get.summary: List objects related to a person (single type, paginated)
get.description:
Returns a paginated list of objects of a specific type related to a person.
Relationships pointing to deleted objects are silently filtered out.
Returns recently seen unique page view names, optionally filtered by a search term.

delete.description: Queues relationship deletion via the ingress pipeline. Same request format as create.

post.description: Queues relationship creation via the ingress pipeline. Accepts a batch of relationships; each must pair a customer (entity1) with an object (entity2) using internal IDs.

get.summary: List customers related to an object
get.description: Returns paginated customers that have a relationship with the specified object, along with the relationship details.

get.summary: List objects related to a person (all types, paginated)
get.description:
Returns a paginated list of objects related to a person across all object types.
Relationships pointing to deleted objects are silently filtered out, so the actual item count may be less than `total`.

get.summary: List objects related to a person (top N per type, for preview)
get.description:
Returns up to 10 related objects per object type for preview/liquid usage.
Does not support pagination. Relationships pointing to deleted objects are silently filtered out.

The `filters` parameter accepts a URL-safe base64-encoded JSON object mapping field names to arrays of allowed values, e.g. `{"kind":["segment","campaign"]}`. Supported filter fields: `kind` (segment, campaign, action, template, newsletter, navigation). Entity-specific fields (e.g. `segment_type`, `campaign_type`, `archived`) are allowed when the corresponding kind is selected.

parameters.name: filters
parameters.description: URL-safe base64-encoded JSON map of filter field names to value arrays, e.g. {"kind":["segment","campaign"]}

parameters.name: test
parameters.description: If non-empty, return test search results

get.description:
Response shape depends on query parameters:
- `short=1`: returns getSegmentsShortResponse (abbreviated segment list)
- `size > 0`: returns getSegmentsPaginatedResponse (full segments with pagination metadata)
- default: returns getSegmentsDefaultResponse (full segments without pagination)

parameters.name: short
parameters.description: Set to 1 for abbreviated segment descriptions

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Filter by name, description, or ID

parameters.name: searchTagIds
parameters.description: JSON array of tag IDs to filter by

parameters.name: archived
parameters.description: Set to true to list archived segments

parameters.name: usage
parameters.description: Filter by usage: in_use, not_in_use, in_sent_newsletters

parameters.name: audience_status
parameters.description: JSON array of audience statuses to filter by

parameters.name: audience_type
parameters.description: JSON array of audience types to filter by

parameters.name: objectTypeIds
parameters.description: JSON array of object type IDs to filter by

put.description: Assigns a subscription topic or send-to-unsubscribed preference to campaigns, newsletters, and campaign actions in bulk.

get.description: Returns up to 10 profiles flagged with cio_test_user=true. Default returns both pending and double-opt-in-verified test users (matches /test_users/count). Pass ?verified=true to narrow to the double-opt-in subset (used by the Builder-plan newsletter recipients UI).
Returns the number of verified test users (cio_test_user=true) in the workspace and the per-workspace cap. Used by the Builders onboarding flow to render remaining test-user spots.
